title: New ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure
description: Security researchers have identified a new ransomware variant specifically designed to target cloud-based infrastructure. The campaign uses compromised CI/CD pipelines to deploy malicious code.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The ransomware campaign targeting cloud infrastructure poses a significant threat to organizations relying on cloud-based services, potentially leading to data loss, service disruption, and financial damage. Compromised CI/CD pipelines could result in widespread deployment of malicious code across production environments.
recommended_actions: Implement strict access controls and monitor CI/CD pipelines for unusual activity to prevent unauthorized code deployment.
Regularly back up critical data and ensure backups are stored in isolated environments to mitigate ransomware impact.
Deploy endpoint detection and response (EDR) solutions and conduct regular threat hunting to identify and remediate potential compromises.

title: Show HN: Open-source vulnerability scanner
description: New tool automatically scans dependencies for known vulnerabilities and suggests patches. Gaining traction in the security community.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The open-source vulnerability scanner itself does not pose a direct threat but could be exploited by malicious actors to identify and target unpatched vulnerabilities in systems. The tool's widespread adoption may also lead to over-reliance without proper validation of its results.
recommended_actions: Evaluate the tool in a controlled environment to ensure it meets organizational security standards before adoption.
Educate teams on the importance of validating scanner results and applying patches responsibly.
Monitor for any updates or reported vulnerabilities in the tool itself to prevent misuse or exploitation.

title: Note Mark has Stored XSS via Unrestricted Asset Upload
description:
### Summary
A stored same-origin XSS vulnerability allows any authenticated user to upload an HTML, SVG, or XHTML file as a note asset and have it executed in a victim’s browser under the application’s origin. Because the application serves these files inline without a safe content type and without `nosniff`, browsers can sniff and render active content, giving the attacker access to authenticated Note Mark API actions as the victim.

### Details
This issue results from three compounding flaws i

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40262.

title: Maddy Mail Server has an LDAP Filter Injection via Unsanitized Username
description:
### Summary

The `auth.ldap` module constructs LDAP search filters and DN strings by directly interpolating user-supplied usernames via `strings.ReplaceAll()` without any LDAP filter escaping. An attacker who can reach the SMTP submission (AUTH PLAIN) or IMAP LOGIN interface can inject arbitrary LDAP filter expressions through the username field, enabling identity spoofing, LDAP directory enumeration, and attribute value extraction. The `go-ldap/ldap/v3` library—already imported in the same file

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40193.

title: FITS GZIP decompression bomb in Pillow
description:
### Impact
Pillow did not limit the amount of GZIP-compressed data read when decoding a FITS image, making it vulnerable to decompression bomb attacks. A specially crafted FITS file could cause unbounded memory consumption, leading to denial of service (OOM crash or severe performance degradation).

### Patches
The amount of data read is now limited to the necessary amount.
Fixed in Pillow 12.2.0 (PR #9521).

### Workarounds
Avoid Pillow >= 10.3.0, < 12.2.0
Only open [specific image formats](htt

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: Potential security vulnerability with high severity. CVE: CVE-2026-40192.

title: ImageMagick has an integer overflow in despeckle operation causing a heap buffer overflow on 32-bit builds
description:
An integer overflow in the despeckle operation causes a heap buffer overflow on 32-bit builds that will result in an out of bounds write.

```
==1551685==ERROR: AddressSanitizer: heap-buffer-overflow on address 0xea2fb818 at pc 0x56cbc42a bp 0xffc4ce48 sp 0xffc4ce38
WRITE of size 8 at 0xea2fb818 thread T0
```

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in ImageMagick's despeckle operation can lead to a heap buffer overflow on 32-bit builds, potentially allowing attackers to execute arbitrary code or cause a denial of service (DoS) by crashing the application.
recommended_actions: Update to the latest patched version of ImageMagick that addresses CVE-2026-34238.
Implement input validation and sanitization to minimize the risk of malicious image files being processed.
Use AddressSanitizer or similar tools during development to detect and mitigate memory-related vulnerabilities.

title: ImageMagick has a heap overflow caused by integer overflow/wraparound in viff encoder on 32-bit builds
description: In viff encoder contains an integer truncation/wraparound issue on 32-bit builds that could trigger an out of bounds heap write that can result in a crash.

Performing a manipulation of the argument Name results in sql injection. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows remote attackers to perform SQL injection, potentially leading to unauthorized access, data exfiltration, or manipulation of sensitive information in the Construction Management System.

title: Use-after-free (UAF) was possible in the `lzma.LZMADecompressor`, `bz2.BZ2Decompressor`, and ...
description:
Use-after-free (UAF) was possible in the `lzma.LZMADecompressor`, `bz2.BZ2Decompressor`, and `gzip.GzipFile` when a memory allocation fails with a `MemoryError` and the decompression instance is re-used. This scenario can be triggered if the process is under memory pressure. The fix cleans up the dangling pointer in this specific error condition.

The vulnerability is only present if the program re-uses decompressor instances across multiple decompression calls even after a `MemoryError` is rais

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability allows for a use-after-free (UAF) condition, which can lead to arbitrary code execution, data corruption, or application crashes. This poses a significant risk in scenarios where decompression libraries are used in memory-constrained environments or where untrusted data is processed.
recommended_actions: Immediately apply the patch or update to the latest version of the affected libraries (`lzma`, `bz2`, `gzip`) where the issue has been resolved.
Audit and refactor code to ensure decompressor instances are not reused after a `MemoryError` is raised.
Implement memory usage monitoring and safeguards to prevent processes from operating under extreme memory pressure.

title: A vulnerability exists in the command handling of the IEC 61850 communication stack included in...
description:
A vulnerability exists in the command handling of the IEC 61850 communication stack included in the product revisions listed as affected in this CVE. An attacker with access to IEC 61850 networks could exploit the vulnera bility by using a specially crafted 61850 packet, forcing the communication interfaces of the PM 877, CI850 and CI868 modules into fault mode or causing unavailability of the S+ Operations 61850 connectivity, resulting in a denial-of-service situation.

recommended_actions: Immediately update or patch the PHPGurukul Daily Expense Tracking System to the latest version if a fix is available.

Monitor systems for unusual activity and consider deploying a web application firewall (WAF) to detect and block SQL injection attempts.

description: A vulnerability has been found in itsourcecode Construction Management System 1.0. title: CVE-2026-5618 - A vulnerability was detected in kalcaddle kodbox up to 1.64. This affects an unknown function of the
description: A vulnerability was detected in kalcaddle kodbox up to 1.64. This affects an unknown function of the component shareMake/shareCheck. Performing a manipulation of the argument siteFrom/siteTo results in server-side request forgery. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The complexity of an attack is rather high. The exploitability is reported as difficult. The exploit is now public and may be used. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure but did not respond in any way.

ai_analysis.impact_assessment: The vulnerability in kalcaddle kodbox up to version 1.64 allows for server-side request forgery (SSRF), which could enable attackers to manipulate server requests to access internal systems, sensitive data, or conduct further attacks. The exploit is public, increasing the likelihood of exploitation despite the high complexity.
affected_industries: Small and Medium Enterprises (SMEs) using kodbox
recommended_actions: Immediately upgrade to a patched version of kalcaddle kodbox if available, or consider replacing it with an alternative solution if the vendor remains unresponsive.
Implement network-level protections such as web application firewalls (WAFs) and restrict outbound server requests to trusted destinations only.
Monitor server logs for unusual or unauthorized requests originating from the vulnerable component and isolate affected systems if suspicious activity is detected.

title: CVE-2026-5616 - A security vulnerability has been detected in JeecgBoot 3.9.0/3.9.1. The impacted element is an unkn
description: A security vulnerability has been detected in JeecgBoot 3.9.0/3.9.1. The impacted element is an unknown function of the file jeecg-boot/jeecg-module-system/jeecg-system-biz/src/main/java/org/jeecg/modules/airag/JeecgBizToolsProvider.java of the component AI Chat Module. Such manipulation leads to missing authentication. The attack can be executed remotely. The name of the patch is b7c9aeba7aefda9e008ea8fe4fc3daf08d0c5b39/2c1cc88b8d983868df8c520a343d6ff4369d9e59. It is best practice to apply a patch to resolve this issue. The project fixed the issue with a commit which shall be part of the next official release.
